conflict_type: Conflicts between Keyword Constraints and Phrase Constraints
seed_instruction: Design a one-year plan for mastering a musical instrument, including practice schedules and milestone goals.
org_constraint: Include the phrase "consistent daily practice" in at least three paragraphs.
new_constraint: The keywords "daily" and "practice" should not appear in the same sentence.
instruction: Design a one-year plan for mastering a musical instrument, including practice schedules and milestone goals. Include the phrase "consistent daily practice" in at least three paragraphs. The keywords "daily" and "practice" should not appear in the same sentence.
response:
To master a musical instrument in one year, you must approach the journey with a structured roadmap that balances technical proficiency with artistic expression. The foundation of this progression relies on **consistent daily practice** to build muscle memory and neurological pathways. By dedicating time every morning or evening, you ensure that your fingers develop the necessary dexterity to navigate complex scales and chords.

The first three months focus on the "Foundations Phase." During this period, your goals are to learn proper posture, basic notation, and fundamental scales. You should aim to complete one new scale and three simple pieces per month. Success in this stage is achieved through **consistent daily practice** that prioritizes accuracy over speed. It is vital to perform these exercises every single day to solidify the basics.

Months four through eight represent the "Intermediate Development Phase." Here, you will transition into more complex repertoire and begin exploring different genres. You should aim to perform a full five-minute piece by the end of the sixth month. To reach this milestone, **consistent daily practice** is required to refine your dynamics and phrasing. You must engage with your instrument on a daily basis to maintain your momentum.

The final four months are the "Artistry and Performance Phase." Your goal is to prepare a 15-minute set of polished pieces for a live audience or a recorded session. Focus on emotional expression and technical fluidity. By the end of the year, you will have transformed from a novice into a capable musician who can perform with confidence and grace.<turn|>

By dedicating time every morning or evening, you ensure that your fingers develop the necessary dexterity to navigate complex scales and chords.

The first three months focus on the "Foundations Phase." During this period, your goals are to learn proper posture, basic notation, and fundamental scales. You should aim to complete one new scale and three simple pieces per month. Success in this stage is achieved through **consistent daily practice** that prioritizes accuracy over speed. It is vital to perform these exercises every single day to solidify the basics.

Months four through eight represent the "Intermediate Development Phase." Here, you will transition into more complex repertoire and begin exploring different genres. You should aim to perform a full five-minute piece by the end of the sixth month. To reach this milestone, **consistent daily practice** is required to refine your dynamics and phrasing. You must engage with your instrument on a daily basis to maintain your momentum.

The final four months are the "Artistry and Performance Phase." Your goal is to prepare a 15-minute set of polished pieces for a live audience or a recorded session.
